expectations.text: Recommends 'nats stream report' and 'nats consumer report' (or 'nats consumer info') as the first diagnostic commands
expectations.evidence:
nats consumer report ORDERS

# Full details on the specific consumer
nats consumer info ORDERS order-processor

expectations.text: Distinguishes between num_pending (messages not yet delivered) and num_ack_pending (delivered but not yet acked) — these have different fixes
expectations.evidence: Num Pending | Messages in the stream not yet delivered to this consumer | The backlog — how far behind the consumer is | Num Ack Pending | Messages delivered to workers but not yet acknowledged | How many in-flight messages the consumer is holding

expectations.text: Mentions MaxAckPending as a potential bottleneck (when it's too low, JetStream stops delivering new messages)
expectations.evidence: If Num Ack Pending equals Max Ack Pending, the consumer is blocked — NATS will not deliver any more messages until workers acknowledge the ones already in flight. This is the single most common cause of growing Num Pending when workers appear to be running.

expectations.text: Mentions AckWait timeout as a cause of redeliveries that can contribute to apparent lag
expectations.evidence: If AckWait is shorter than the time your workers need to process a message, NATS redelivers the message before the worker finishes. This wastes capacity: the worker processes the message, acks it, but NATS has already moved on to a redelivery — wasting slots and causing double-processing.

expectations.text: Provides at least 3 distinct root causes with specific fixes for each (not just a generic list)
expectations.evidence: Cause 1: MaxAckPending Too Low (fix: nats consumer edit ... --max-pending 5000); Cause 2: AckWait Too Short (fix: nats consumer edit ... --ack-wait 120s); Cause 3: Fetch Batch Size Too Small (fix: increase Fetch batch size in worker code); Cause 4: Not Enough Worker Instances; Cause 5: Cluster or Leader Issue

expectations.text: Provides the specific 'nats consumer info <stream> <consumer>' CLI command for detailed inspection
expectations.evidence: nats consumer info ORDERS order-processor

expectations.text: Uses LimitsPolicy or InterestPolicy retention (not WorkQueuePolicy, which destroys messages after one ack — wrong for fanout)
expectations.evidence: Retention: nats.LimitsPolicy

expectations.text: Creates a separate durable consumer for each of the 3 services: payment, inventory, and notifications
expectations.evidence: Durable: "payment-service" ... Durable: "inventory-service" ... Durable: "notifications-service"

expectations.text: Uses AckExplicit (not AckAll or AckNone) for the consumer ack policy
expectations.evidence: AckPolicy: nats.AckExplicit

expectations.text: Sets MaxDeliver or mentions dead-letter handling strategy for failed messages
expectations.evidence: MaxDeliver: 5 (payment), MaxDeliver: 7 (inventory), MaxDeliver: 3 (notifications); msg.Term() for unrecoverable errors

expectations.text: Provides working Go code (not pseudocode) using nats.go — includes actual function calls like nc.Connect, js.AddStream, etc.
expectations.evidence: nc, err := nats.Connect(natsURL, ...) ... js, err := nc.JetStream(...) ... _, err := js.AddStream(cfg) ... js.AddConsumer("ORDERS", c.config)

expectations.text: Uses a hierarchical subject namespace like 'orders.>' or 'orders.created' (not a flat subject like 'orders')
expectations.evidence: Subjects: []string{"orders.>"} ... Subject: "orders.created"

expectations.text: Provides a values.yaml with JetStream explicitly enabled (jetstream.enabled: true or equivalent)
expectations.evidence:
jetstream:
    enabled: true
    memoryStore:
      enabled: true
      size: 4Gi

expectations.text: Includes resource requests and limits for the NATS containers
expectations.evidence:
resources:
    requests:
      cpu: "2"
      memory: 8Gi
    limits:
      cpu: "4"
      memory: 16Gi

expectations.text: Includes readiness and liveness probes (or references port 8222 health endpoint)
expectations.evidence:
readinessProbe:
    httpGet:
      path: /healthz?js-enabled-only=true
      port: 8222 ... livenessProbe:
    httpGet:
      path: /healthz
      port: 8222

expectations.text: Includes a NetworkPolicy manifest restricting ingress/egress
expectations.evidence:
kind: NetworkPolicy ... policyTypes:
    - Ingress
    - Egress ... ingress: [client port 4222, cluster route 6222, monitoring 8222/7777] ... egress: [cluster route 6222, DNS 53]

expectations.text: References TLS configuration (certificate secret or TLS settings in values.yaml)
expectations.evidence:
tls:
    secret:
      name: nats-tls
    ca: "ca.pem"
    cert: "server-cert.pem"
    key: "server-key.pem"
    verify: true

expectations.text: References NKey or JWT authentication (not plain token auth)
expectations.evidence:
authorization:
      users:
        - nkey: "UA7BQBSMZORDER000EXAMPLEPUBKEY" ... - nkey: "UB3DKQMSLANALYTICS000EXAMPLEPUBKEY"

expectations.text: Includes persistent volume claim or storage config for JetStream data
expectations.evidence:
fileStore:
      enabled: true
      size: 100Gi
      storageClassName: gp3-encrypted

expectations.text: Recommends 'nats stream report' and 'nats consumer report' (or 'nats consumer info') as the first diagnostic commands
expectations.evidence: Step 1 opens with 'nats consumer report <stream> <consumer> --watch' and 'nats consumer info <stream> <consumer>' but uses 'nats stream info' — 'nats stream report' is never mentioned anywhere in the document

expectations.text: Distinguishes between num_pending (messages not yet delivered) and num_ack_pending (delivered but not yet acked) — these have different fixes
expectations.evidence: Step 1 lists 'Num Ack Pending — messages delivered but not yet acknowledged' separately from Num Pending; Root Cause 1 targets MaxAckPending cap with 'increase --max-pending' while Root Cause 3 targets general throughput with worker scaling — distinct fixes for each

expectations.text: Mentions MaxAckPending as a potential bottleneck (when it's too low, JetStream stops delivering new messages)
expectations.evidence: 'Max Ack Pending — the in-flight cap; if workers hit this, delivery stalls' and Root Cause 1: 'Once the in-flight cap is hit, JetStream stops delivering new messages'

expectations.text: Mentions AckWait timeout as a cause of redeliveries that can contribute to apparent lag
expectations.evidence: Root Cause 2 'Ack Wait Too Short — Redelivery Storm': 'Workers receive a message, begin processing, but take longer than Ack Wait. JetStream redelivers the message to another worker (or the same one), doubling the work. This cascades.'

expectations.text: Provides at least 3 distinct root causes with specific fixes for each (not just a generic list)
expectations.evidence: Seven numbered root causes are provided, each with specific CLI commands or code fixes, e.g. Root Cause 1: 'nats consumer edit <stream> <consumer> --max-pending 10000', Root Cause 2: '--ack-wait 300s' plus Go code for msg.InProgress(), Root Cause 3: worker scaling and fetch batch size tuning

expectations.text: Provides the specific 'nats consumer info <stream> <consumer>' CLI command for detailed inspection
expectations.evidence: 'nats consumer info <stream> <consumer>' appears in Step 1 as a standalone command with key fields to inspect, and again in Step 2: 'nats consumer info <stream> <consumer> | grep -E "Ack Pending|Max Ack Pending"'

expectations.text: Uses LimitsPolicy or InterestPolicy retention (not WorkQueuePolicy, which destroys messages after one ack — wrong for fanout)
expectations.evidence: Retention: nats.LimitsPolicy

expectations.text: Creates a separate durable consumer for each of the 3 services: payment, inventory, and notifications
expectations.evidence: Durable: "payment-service" ... Durable: "inventory-service" ... Durable: "notifications-service"

expectations.text: Uses AckExplicit (not AckAll or AckNone) for the consumer ack policy
expectations.evidence: AckPolicy: nats.AckExplicitPolicy

expectations.text: Sets MaxDeliver or mentions dead-letter handling strategy for failed messages
expectations.evidence: MaxDeliver: 5 (payment, inventory) and MaxDeliver: 3 (notifications); plus operational note: "When MaxDeliver is exhausted JetStream stops retrying. Add a separate consumer... as an audit log"

expectations.text: Provides working Go code (not pseudocode) using nats.go — includes actual function calls like nc.Connect, js.AddStream, etc.
expectations.evidence: nc, err := nats.Connect(nats.DefaultURL) ... jsc, err := nc.JetStream() ... js.AddStream(&nats.StreamConfig{...}) ... js.AddConsumer(StreamName, cfg) ... jsc.PullSubscribe("orders.>", durableName, ...)

expectations.text: Uses a hierarchical subject namespace like 'orders.>' or 'orders.created' (not a flat subject like 'orders')
expectations.evidence: Subjects: []string{"orders.>"} ... jsc.Publish("orders.created", payload, ...) ... FilterSubject: "orders.>"

expectations.text: Provides a values.yaml with JetStream explicitly enabled (jetstream.enabled: true or equivalent)
expectations.evidence:
nats:
  jetstream:
    enabled: true
    memStorage:
      enabled: true
      size: 1Gi
    fileStorage:
      enabled: true
